underlying: 2026 Ballon d'Or winner (men's award, ceremony Oct/Nov 2026, Paris)
resolution_time: 2026-12-31T04:59:00Z (Kalshi close); actual award ~late Oct or early Nov 2026 — early close
method: Categorical prior across 30 listed candidates. Anchored on (a) Kalshi mid-quotes (last prices), (b) bookmaker/Polymarket aggregate, (c) media power-rankings (Goal, OneFootball, Football365, GiveMeSport, OLBG). The 2026 race is unusually volatile: 2025 winner Dembele's PSG were eliminated from UCL, Mbappe's Real Madrid eliminated, Yamal's Barcelona eliminated. Bayern duo Kane (33 goals incl 32 in Bundesliga = European Golden Shoe leader) and Olise (18G/29A in 45 matches) lead media power-rankings. World Cup 2026 (June-July) will heavily skew final voting — most contenders need a deep WC run. Probabilities below combine market signals with a slight regression to the mean (small lifts to top non-frontrunners and to long-tail OTHER, small fade on Kane to reflect WC variance — Kane has chronic England knockout-stage failure history). Kalshi sum-of-yes ~ 96 cents (typical bid-side underpricing); I normalize to 1.00 and add 'undisclosed/other player' implicit bucket via slightly raising long-tail floor (each minor candidate >= 0.005). Final probabilities sum to 1.00 across the 30 listed strikes. Vegas (best price) snapshot: Yamal 11/4 (~26%), Kane 4/1 (~20%), Mbappe 4/1 (~20%), Olise 17%, Dembele 5% — but Kalshi traders see less Yamal (Barca exited UCL, hurts) and more Kane (Bundesliga golden shoe locked in). I split the difference but lean to Kalshi for live tape.
notes: Probabilities sum to ~1.000 (verified). Market mid-quotes were: Kane 0.345, Olise 0.145, Mbappe 0.115, Dembele 0.095, Yamal 0.065, Vitinha 0.035, Rice 0.025, Haaland 0.015, all others ≤0.01. Market sum ≈ 0.92 (typical bid-side under-1 due to no-side overpricing). Adjustments from market: (1) Kane 0.345→0.27: Kane has ZERO England trophies and Bayern got knocked out of UCL semis — historical voter pattern penalizes pure goal-scorers without team trophies (e.g., Lewandowski 2021 robbery, Haaland 2023 lost to Messi). Also massive 2026 WC variance — England loses early, Kane drops. (2) Yamal 0.065→0.10: Barca UCL exit hurts but Yamal at 18 has narrative-driver advantage; if Spain wins WC he becomes near-lock. Kalshi underprices given youth voters split. (3) Mbappe held ~0.115: France WC favorites + La Liga golden boot competitive. (4) Olise 0.145→0.13: Bayern Bundesliga + UCL run is real but assists narrative is harder sell vs goals. (5) Dembele 0.095→0.10: defending champion, slight boost — voter recency bias is real. (6) Rice 0.025→0.035: Arsenal had strong UCL run + England captain candidate. CRITICAL: ~10% probability mass is in 'unlisted other' world (e.g., Vinicius if Real wins LaLiga + WC) which I distribute across long tail to keep market-tradeable strikes covering 1.00 sum. Long-dated (6+ months out, with WC mid-cycle) — confidence LOW.
